commands.review_summary:
The PR changes voice-call realtime WebSocket upgrade matching from raw prefix routing to exact-or-slash-child path routing and adds sibling/root route-boundary coverage.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +204. Total +215 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main and v2026.6.5 use `pathname.startsWith(pattern)`, so `/voice/stream/realtime-evil/token` classifies under `/voice/stream/realtime`; I did not run a failing current-main scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to accept the compatibility impact and choose the canonical fix path; no narrow automated code repair is needed.

target.title: fix(voice-call): require realtime websocket path boundary
target.body:
Summary:
- Tightens realtime voice WebSocket upgrade routing so same-prefix sibling paths are not routed to the realtime handler.
- Previously, `/voice/stream/realtime-evil/token` matched `/voice/stream/realtime` because the matcher used raw `startsWith(pattern)` matching.
- The matcher now accepts only the exact configured path or a real slash-delimited child path under it.
- Preserves the existing root `realtime.streamPath="/"` behavior so root child paths such as `/token` still route to the realtime handler.

Validation:
- Added regression coverage for sibling realtime WebSocket child paths in `extensions/voice-call/src/webhook.test.ts`.
- Added regression coverage for root realtime WebSocket child paths in `extensions/voice-call/src/webhook.test.ts`.
- Added focused route-boundary proof coverage in `extensions/voice-call/src/live-realtime-route-proof.test.ts`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook.test.ts`
  - 42 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts`
  - 61 passed

Behavior proof:
- The committed webhook regression tests start a real `VoiceCallWebhookServer` on an ephemeral local port, send Node HTTP upgrade requests to the bound server, and assert whether the realtime upgrade handler was reached.
- The committed focused proof test exercises the realtime route predicate for the three route-boundary cases:
  - sibling prefix rejected: `/voice/stream/realtime-evil/token` against `/voice/stream/realtime` -> `false`
  - real child accepted: `/voice/stream/realtime/token` against `/voice/stream/realtime` -> `true`
  - root child preserved: `/token` against `/` -> `true`

Proof command:
```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/voice-call/src/live-realtime-route-proof.test.ts
```

Expected proof log shape:
```text
[route-proof] pattern=/voice/stream/realtime path=/voice/stream/realtime-evil/token actual=false expected=false
[route-proof] pattern=/voice/stream/realtime path=/voice/stream/realtime/token actual=true expected=true
[route-proof] pattern=/ path=/token actual=true expected=true
```

Note:
- This PR no longer claims a nonexistent `live-realtime-route-proof.test.ts` run without committing the file. The proof file is now included in the branch.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
